title: Podman does not remove old container during update, causing network issues with duplicate container names
body:
### Issue Description

When updating container to a newer version which uses different image registry in Quadlet file, the update brake network.

**Suspected reason:**
The new container does not join the old network. Instead, it defaults to the Podman default network, which does not have internet access. 

**Observed behavior:**
- During the update, Podman does not remove the old container after pulling the new image from the new registry.
- As a result, two containers with the same name coexist in the system.
- This causes Podman's network management logic to break: the "second" container fails to join the intended network.
- The problem is not logged anywhere, making it hard to detect.

Rolling back to the old container version resolves the issue, suggesting that Podman's container handling during image updates is not fully reliable in this case.

**Temporary workaround:**
Manually delete all containers downloaded from Docker Hub using the CLI before updating:

```
podman rm <old_container_id>
```

**Environment:**

```
Client: Podman Engine
Version: 4.9.3
API Version: 4.9.3
Go Version: go1.22.2
Built: Thu Jan 1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch: linux/arm64
```

comments.body:
A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

We only support the latest version upstream so please test with that. And your reproducer is unclear, please provide exact steps on what you are doing.
It is impossible to have two containers with the same name at the same time so I don't see how this would ever happen.
Not is there any time of update when you pull a new image, you delete and recreate the containers always.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
